tagline: Work with GitLab projects from the command line.

global_flags.name: connection
global_flags.summary: Use a saved GitLab connector credential and base URL scope

global_flags.name: limit
global_flags.summary: Limit records emitted by stream-backed commands

flags.name: search
flags.summary: Filter projects by search text when the stream runner allows query overrides.

flags.name: owned
flags.summary: Filter to projects owned by the authenticated account when supported by GitLab.

flags.name: search
flags.summary: Filter groups by search text when the stream runner allows query overrides.

commands.summary: List GitLab users visible to the token

flags.name: search
flags.summary: Filter users by search text when the stream runner allows query overrides.

flags.name: username
flags.summary: Filter by username when supported by GitLab.

commands.summary: List GitLab issues visible to the token

flags.name: state
flags.summary: Filter issues by state when supported by GitLab.

flags.name: assignee-username
flags.summary: Filter issues by assignee username when supported by GitLab.

flags.name: label
flags.summary: Filter issues by label names when supported by GitLab.

commands.notes: Bounded direct read of one project; response is recursively redacted before output.

flags.name: id
flags.summary: Numeric GitLab project ID. Path-style project identifiers are intentionally not accepted by this direct-read mapping.

commands.notes: Bounded direct read of one group; response is recursively redacted before output.

flags.name: id
flags.summary: Numeric GitLab group ID. Path-style group identifiers are intentionally not accepted by this direct-read mapping.

commands.summary: View events for a GitLab user
commands.notes: Bounded direct read of a user event page; response is recursively redacted before output.
Bounded direct read of one project issue; response is recursively redacted before output.

commands.risk: Would create a visible issue in a GitLab project.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Would mutate issue title, description, labels, assignees, or state.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Would change issue state in a GitLab project.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Would change issue state in a GitLab project.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Deletes project data and may be irreversible.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; destructive writes need an explicit policy and typed confirmation before dispatch.
commands.notes: Destructive issue deletion is not exposed by this metadata slice.

commands.summary: Add a note to an issue
commands.risk: Would create a visible note on an issue.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.
Merge request stream coverage belongs to a future stream expansion or operation-ledger lane.
Single merge-request lookup requires bounded direct-read support.

commands.risk: Would create a visible merge request in a project.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Would mutate merge request metadata or state.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Merges code into the target branch and can trigger CI/CD or deployments.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; destructive or deployment-adjacent writes need explicit policy and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Not exposed until sensitive/admin policy and typed confirmation are implemented.

commands.risk: Would record a review approval on a merge request.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.
Pipelines are ETL candidates but are not current streams.
Pipeline detail reads require direct-read operation metadata.

commands.risk: Starts CI/CD execution and may deploy or mutate environments.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; execution requires plan, preview, approval, and typed confirmation policy.
commands.notes: No pipeline-trigger write action is exposed by this metadata slice.

commands.risk: Would stop CI/CD execution for a project pipeline.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.
Job reads are bounded direct-read candidates.
Binary downloads require explicit size limits and output destination policy before enabling.
Schedule stream coverage is deferred.

commands.risk: Starts scheduled CI/CD execution and may deploy or mutate environments.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; execution requires plan, preview, approval, and typed confirmation policy.
commands.notes: Not exposed by this metadata slice.
Runner inventory often requires elevated scope and is deferred to the operation ledger.

commands.risk: Changes CI execution infrastructure and may introduce privileged compute.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; admin infrastructure writes need explicit policy and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Not exposed by this metadata slice.
Requires a constrained local git executor and destination path policy; not a connector API dispatch.
Binary archive downloads require explicit size and output-path policy before enabling.

commands.risk: Would create a project and allocate namespace resources.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Would mutate project settings.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Deletes a project and its repository data.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; destructive admin writes need explicit policy and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Repository deletion is intentionally not exposed.

commands.summary: Transfer a GitLab project to another namespace
commands.risk: Changes project ownership and namespace access boundaries.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; admin writes need explicit policy and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Not exposed by this metadata slice.
Label stream coverage is deferred.

commands.risk: Would create a label in a project or group.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Deletes a label and may affect issue or merge request workflows.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; destructive writes need explicit policy and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Not exposed by this metadata slice.
Milestone stream coverage is deferred.
Release stream coverage is deferred.

commands.risk: Would publish a release in a GitLab project.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.
Binary asset downloads require explicit size limits and output destination policy before enabling.
Snippet streams are deferred to the operation ledger.

commands.risk: Would create project or personal snippet content.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.
To-do items are user-scoped direct-read candidates.

commands.risk: Would mutate a user-scoped to-do item.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: No GitLab write action is declared yet.

commands.risk: Variable metadata may be sensitive even when values are masked or hidden.
commands.notes: Requires sensitive-field redaction policy before enabling.

commands.summary: Create or update a CI/CD variable
commands.risk: Writes secret or deployment-affecting configuration.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; sensitive writes require stdin/env input, preview redaction, approval, and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Never request or store variable values in prompts or metadata.

commands.risk: Deletes secret or deployment-affecting configuration.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; destructive sensitive writes require explicit policy and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Not exposed by this metadata slice.

commands.risk: Deploy key metadata can reveal access configuration.
commands.notes: Requires operation-ledger classification and redaction policy before enabling.

commands.risk: Grants repository access to a key.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; access-control writes require explicit policy and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Not exposed by this metadata slice.

commands.risk: Account key metadata can be sensitive.
commands.notes: Requires redaction and account-scope policy before enabling.

commands.risk: Grants account access to a key.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; access-control writes require explicit policy and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Not exposed by this metadata slice.
Account-scoped key metadata requires direct-read policy before enabling.

commands.summary: List personal, project, or group tokens
commands.risk: Token metadata is sensitive and may require elevated scope.
commands.notes: Blocked until sensitive/admin policy defines risk tiers and redaction.

commands.risk: Mutates credentials and can break automation or reveal newly issued secret material.
commands.approval: Blocked by default; credential lifecycle writes require human-approved policy.
commands.notes: Not exposed by this metadata slice.

commands.risk: Secure files may contain signing keys or other sensitive binary content.
commands.notes: Blocked until sensitive binary-output policy exists.

commands.risk: Downloads sensitive binary material to local storage.
commands.notes: Blocked until bounded executor, destination policy, and approval policy exist.

commands.summary: List container registry repositories or tags
commands.notes: Registry inventory is an ETL candidate, deferred to operation ledger classification.
Package registry inventory is an ETL candidate, deferred to operation ledger classification.
Search is a bounded direct-read candidate and must not become a raw API escape hatch.

commands.summary: Make an arbitrary GitLab API request
commands.risk: Arbitrary API dispatch can bypass connector safety, approval, redaction, and operation-ledger classification.
commands.approval: Not exposed. Add typed operations instead of raw API access.
commands.notes: Generic raw HTTP reads/writes are intentionally disallowed.
Polymetrics credentials are managed through pm credential flows and never through prompt text.
Local glab configuration is outside the GitLab connector.
Local alias configuration is outside connector execution.
Shell completion generation is handled by the pm CLI, not by the GitLab connector surface.
Changelog generation combines reads and local formatting; it needs an explicit bounded workflow before exposure.

commands.summary: List GitLab Agents for Kubernetes clusters
commands.risk: Cluster metadata can be sensitive and may require elevated scope.
commands.notes: Deferred to operation-ledger and sensitive/admin policy lanes.
Interactive AI assistant behavior is outside connector ETL/reverse ETL scope.
Local server processes are outside connector command dispatch.

commands.summary: Work with OpenTofu or Terraform integration state
commands.risk: Infrastructure state can contain sensitive data and environment topology.
commands.notes: Requires explicit operation classification and redaction policy before enabling.
Stacked-diff workflow requires local git operations and is outside connector API dispatch.

help_topics.name: authentication
help_topics.summary: Use saved Polymetrics credentials or environment/stdin-based credential loading; never pass token values in prompts.

help_topics.name: writes
help_topics.summary: GitLab write-like commands are either planned reverse ETL operations or blocked-by-default safety surfaces; planned writes must use reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, execute before dispatch.

help_topics.name: binary-downloads
help_topics.summary: Artifacts, archives, secure files, and release assets remain disabled until bounded size and output destination policies exist.

help_topics.name: raw-api
help_topics.summary: Generic raw API commands are intentionally disallowed; use typed streams, direct reads, or future typed write actions instead.
